title: Reed Workflow
description: Reed workflow definition file (.yml/.yaml). Defines a local-first, daemonless workflow with triggers, jobs, steps, agents, skills, and MCP servers. Note: the parser silently ignores unknown fields (no strict-mode rejection), so this schema does not set additionalProperties: false.

tool_access.description: Tool filesystem access scope. "workdir" restricts tools to the working directory; "full" allows unrestricted access.

run_jobs.description: Default DAG entrypoint filter. Each item must reference an existing job ID.

inputs.description: Input parameter definitions. Note: type validation is only enforced for trigger-scoped inputs (on.service.http, on.service.mcp, on.cli.commands), not for workflow-level inputs.

outputs.description: Output expressions. The validator rejects expressions that contain both "jobs." and ".steps" (cross-job step piercing), but does not catch bare "steps." references at workflow scope.

env.description: Environment variables available to all jobs and steps.

agents.description: Agent role definitions. Each key is a unique agent ID referenced by steps via with.agent.

skills.description: Skill definitions. Referenced by agent specs via their skills array.

mcp_servers.description: MCP server connections (Reed as client). Each key is a unique server ID.

jobs.description: Job DAG. Must contain at least one job. Each key becomes the job ID.

metadata.description: Opaque metadata. Passed through without validation.

OnSpec.description: Trigger configuration. If omitted or empty (on: {}), defaults to implicit CLI trigger (on: { cli: {} }). cli and service are mutually exclusive. cli and schedule are mutually exclusive. service and schedule can coexist.

CLITrigger.description: Ephemeral batch mode execution with optional subcommands.

commands.description: Named subcommands. Each key is the command name.

CLICommand.description: Named subcommand within a CLI trigger.

run_jobs.description: Job IDs to run for this command. Must reference existing jobs.

ServiceTrigger.description: Daemon mode execution with HTTP and/or MCP routes. If both http and mcp are empty, a default POST / route is auto-injected.

port.description: Listen port. Required and must be > 0.

HTTPRoute.description: HTTP trigger within a service workflow.

method.description: HTTP method. Validated against: GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE, HEAD, OPTIONS. The validator uppercases before checking, so lowercase values like "post" are accepted.

async.description: If true, the request returns immediately and the workflow runs asynchronously.

run_jobs.description: Job IDs to run. Must reference existing jobs.

ConcurrencySpec.description: Defines how concurrent runs of the same route should be handled.

group.description: Concurrency partition key. Typically an expression like "${{ inputs.session_id }}". Must not be empty or whitespace-only.

behavior.description: How a new request is handled when the same group is busy. "queue" = strict FIFO (default). "skip" = reject the new request. "replace-pending" = keep running request, retain only newest pending.

MCPTool.description: MCP tool trigger within a service workflow.

name.description: Tool name. Must not be empty.

run_jobs.description: Job IDs to run. Must reference existing jobs.

cron.description: 5-field cron expression: minute hour day-of-month month day-of-week. Example: "0 9 * * *" (daily at 9am).

run_jobs.description: Job IDs to run. Must reference existing jobs.

type.description: Input value type. Note: type validation is only enforced for trigger-scoped inputs, not workflow-level inputs.

required.description: Whether this input must be provided.

default.description: Default value if the input is not provided. Type should match the declared type.

AgentSpec.description: Agent role definition with model, system prompt, skills, and control parameters.

model.description: LLM model identifier. Can be an expression like "${{ env.REED_DEFAULT_MODEL ?? 'gpt-5.4' }}".

system_prompt.description: System prompt for the agent. Supports multi-line YAML strings.

skills.description: List of skill IDs to mount for this agent. Each must reference a defined skill.

max_iterations.description: Maximum conversation iterations. 0 = use system default.

temperature.description: LLM temperature parameter. Omit to use model default.

thinking_level.description: Extended thinking level. Omit to use model default.

SkillSpec.description: Skill definition. Exactly one of "uses" or "resources" must be provided.

uses.description: Skill catalog reference. Mutually exclusive with resources.

resources.description: Inline skill resources. Mutually exclusive with uses. Must include exactly one root-level SKILL.md. Paths must be relative, no traversal (..), no duplicates, no nested SKILL.md.

SkillResourceSpec.description: Inline skill resource file. Path must be relative (no absolute paths, no .. traversal). The resource set must include exactly one root-level SKILL.md.

path.description: Relative file path. Must not be absolute or contain traversal. Nested SKILL.md (e.g., "subdir/SKILL.md") is rejected.

content.description: Resource file content. For SKILL.md: YAML frontmatter (name, description, allowed_tools) + markdown body.

MCPServerSpec.description: MCP server connection configuration (Reed as client).

command.description: Command to execute. Required when transport is "stdio".

url.description: Server URL. Required when transport is "sse" or "streamable-http". The validator only checks for non-empty string (no URI format enforcement).

env.description: Environment variables for the server process.

Job.description: Unit of work within the workflow DAG. The job ID is set from the map key.

needs.description: Job IDs that must complete before this job starts. No cycles or self-references allowed.

steps.description: Ordered list of steps to execute. Must contain at least one step.

Step.description: Single executable action within a job.

id.description: Step identifier. Auto-generated as step_0, step_1, etc. if omitted. Note: postProcess auto-fills IDs before validation, so the background+id rule is effectively a dead check in the normal load path.

uses.description: Worker type. Registered workers: "shell", "bash", "run" (all route to ShellWorker), "agent" (AgentWorker), "http" (HTTPWorker). Remote URLs, fragment (#) and registry (@) references are rejected by the validator.

run.description: Shell command (DSL sugar). Promoted to with.run during postProcess. Note: this does NOT auto-set uses; you must set uses to "shell", "bash", or "run" explicitly, otherwise the router will fail with "unknown uses".

with.description: Worker parameters. Contents depend on the worker type. For shell: { run: "..." }. For agent: { agent: "<agent_id>", prompt: "..." }. The validator only checks that with.agent references a defined agent if present; with.prompt is not validated at compile time.

if.description: Conditional expression. Step is skipped if the expression evaluates to false. Uses ${{ ... }} syntax.

timeout.description: Timeout in seconds. 0 = use system default.

workdir.description: Working directory override for this step.

shell.description: Shell identifier. Only valid when uses is "shell", "bash", "run", or empty. uses: bash does not allow shell override (must be "bash" or omitted).

background.description: Run step in background. The validator requires an explicit id when background is true, though postProcess auto-fills IDs before validation in the normal load path.
